key_insight: PREDIKTOR predicts patient-specific drug response by aligning a personalized tumor network view with a simulated drug-induced transcriptomic change in a shared latent space.
clinical_relevance: This could help match cancer patients to therapies using pre-treatment data while also providing mechanistic, interpretable signals about why a drug may work or fail.
method: Builds a patient-specific gene regulatory network from tumor expression and adds drug-target links from DrugBank.
Uses a frozen gene-gene attention model pretrained on LINCS L1000 to simulate post-treatment expression changes.
Aligns both views with a CLIP-style contrastive objective, then classifies response from the combined embedding.
evidence_signal: The model outperformed state-of-the-art baselines on TCGA across patient-, drug-, and tissue-split evaluations and transferred zero-shot to I-SPY2 with a 5.6% AUROC gain.
watch_item: Because one view is pretrained and the other relies on inferred network structure, real-world deployment may depend on robustness across cohorts and the quality of input transcriptomic data.
paper_title: Predicting Therapeutic Outcome via Aligning Patient-Specific Knowledge Graph and Gene-Level Perturbation Representations
